title: Component Patterns
summary: Component patterns are common, proven ways to structure components.
They solve problems like sharing logic or building flexible pieces.
Learning them saves you reinventing solutions.
You have already used some without naming them.

sections.text: As you build more components, the same structural problems come up again and again. How do you share logic between components, or build one flexible enough to be reused everywhere? Component patterns are the well known, reusable answers.

sections.title: What Component Patterns Are
sections.text: A component pattern is a tried and tested way to arrange components to solve a common problem. For example, one pattern keeps a component purely about how things look, while a parent handles the data and logic. Another builds flexible components that accept content from outside through props. These shapes recur across projects.

sections.title: Why They Help
sections.text: You do not have to invent a structure from scratch each time. When you recognize that a problem fits a known pattern, you can reach for the proven solution and other developers instantly understand it. Many patterns you have likely already used, like passing content into a component through props, you just did not have a name for them.

media.alt: A data holding parent paired with a display only child component

sections.title: Standard Joints in Carpentry
sections.text: Component patterns are like the standard joints a carpenter knows. Rather than inventing a new way to connect two pieces of wood each time, they reach for a proven joint that fits the job. The joints are named and shared, so any carpenter recognizes them. Patterns give developers the same shared vocabulary.

media.alt: A carpenter choosing a proven joint instead of inventing a new one

sections.title: Using Component Patterns
items: Notice a structural problem you keep facing.
Ask if a known pattern already solves it.
Reach for the proven shape instead of inventing one.
Build a vocabulary of patterns over time.

sections.text: You have already used patterns without realizing. Splitting a component into one that holds data and one that just displays it is a classic pattern, and you likely did it by instinct.

terms.definition: A proven way to structure components.
Able to be applied across many projects.
The behavior a pattern may help share.
How flexible components accept outside content.
Shared names developers use for patterns.
